leadText: Not to be confused with Meteonite. The Meteorite (Japanese: いんせき Meteorite) is a Key Item introduced in Generation III. It is used in a variety of contexts in the core series games: In Pokémon Ruby, Sapphire, Emerald, FireRed, and LeafGreen, it has no effect on its own, instead being an item that the player can deliver to an NPC for a reward. In Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, it is fed to Rayquaza during the Delta Episode to enable it to Mega Evolve. In Pokémon Scarlet and Violet, it allows Deoxys to change between its four forms.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation III 1.2 Generation VI 1.3 Generation IX Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation III [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Ruby, Sapphire , and Emerald , the player can return the Meteorite to Professor Cozmo in his house in Fallarbor Town in exchange for TM27 ( Return ). In Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen , the player can give the Meteorite to Lostelle 's father at the Joyful Game Corner on Two Island in exchange for a Moon Stone after rescuing Lostelle at the Berry Forest . Generation VI [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire , the Meteorite changes in appearance and description as the player progresses through the game's story. It transforms from its default form to its second form after the player obtains the Devon Scope on Route 120 . It transforms from its second form to its third form after the player defeats Brendan / May in Lilycove City . It transforms from its third form to its final form after the player defeats Zinnia in Granite Cave . At the climax of the Delta Episode , the player feeds it to Rayquaza to give it the necessary energy to Mega Evolve . In Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, no Rayquaza can Mega Evolve until the Rayquaza in the story has consumed the Meteorite (there is no similar requirement in Generation VII ). Default Second Third Final Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Scarlet and Violet , when the Meteorite is used from the Bag on Deoxys, the player can switch between any of Deoxys's forms in a set order. It changes in the order of: Normal Forme to Attack Forme, Attack Forme to Defense Forme, Defense Forme to Speed Forme, and Speed Forme back to Normal Forme.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Generation III item Games Description R S E A meteorite found at Meteor Falls . FR LG A meteorite that fell from space onto Mt. Moon long ago. It is very lumpy and hard. OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA Default form: A Meteorite originally found at Meteor Falls. You got it at Mt. Chimney. Second form: A Meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. It gives off a faint glow. Third form: A Meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. It continues to give off a faint glow and is slightly warm to the touch. Final form: A Meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. Some sort of pattern has risen to the surface, and it's shining with seven colors of light. S V ZA Default form: A meteorite originally found at Meteor Falls. You got it at Mt. Chimney. Second form: A meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. It gives off a faint glow. Third form: A meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. It continues to give off a faint glow and is slightly warm to the touch. Final form: A meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. Some sort of pattern has risen to the surface, and it's shining with rainbow-colored light. Generation IX item Games Description S V ZA A rock that fell to earth from space. It’s slightly warm to the touch. It allows a certain species of Pokémon to change forms.

Default Second Third Final Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Scarlet and Violet , when the Meteorite is used from the Bag on Deoxys, the player can switch between any of Deoxys's forms in a set order. It changes in the order of: Normal Forme to Attack Forme, Attack Forme to Defense Forme, Defense Forme to Speed Forme, and Speed Forme back to Normal Forme. It is very lumpy and hard. OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA Default form: A Meteorite originally found at Meteor Falls. You got it at Mt. Chimney. Second form: A Meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. It gives off a faint glow. Third form: A Meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. It continues to give off a faint glow and is slightly warm to the touch. Final form: A Meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. Some sort of pattern has risen to the surface, and it's shining with seven colors of light. S V ZA Default form: A meteorite originally found at Meteor Falls. You got it at Mt. Chimney. Second form: A meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. It gives off a faint glow. Third form: A meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. It continues to give off a faint glow and is slightly warm to the touch. Final form: A meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. Some sort of pattern has risen to the surface, and it's shining with rainbow-colored light. Generation IX item Games Description S V ZA A rock that fell to earth from space. It’s slightly warm to the touch. It allows a certain species of Pokémon to change forms. Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Method R S E Mt. Chimney (inside the machine next to Maxie R E /Archie S battle) FR LG Pokémon Network Center (from Bill after arriving for the first time) OR AS Mt. Chimney (from Maxie OR / Archie AS after defeating him) S V Version 3.0.0+ Porto Marinada auction (if the player owns a Deoxys ) Non-item appearances [ edit | edit source ] Since Generation IV , Meteorites have appeared as interactive objects in certain locations, starting in Pokémon Diamond and Pearl . Interacting with these meteorites while Deoxys is in your party can change its form. Some meteorites allow Deoxys to change into one specific form, whereas others allow Deoxys to cycle between all four of its forms, in the same way as the Key Item. Region Location Information Sinnoh Veilstone City Four meteorites in the southeastern corner of the city, in three craters. Each meteorite can change all Deoxys in the player's party into one of its forms: The meteorite in the uppermost crater transforms Deoxys into its Speed Forme. The meteorite in the center crater transforms Deoxys into its Normal Forme. The upper-right meteorite in the lowermost crater transforms Deoxys into its Attack Forme. The lower-left meteorite in the lowermost crater transforms Deoxys into its Defense Forme. Kanto HG SS Route 3 Four meteorites at the southwestern end of the route. Each meteorite can change all Deoxys in the player's party into one of its forms: The top-left meteorite transforms Deoxys into its Normal Forme. The bottom-left meteorite transforms Deoxys into its Defense Forme. The bottom-right meteorite transforms Deoxys into its Speed Forme. The top-right meteorite transforms Deoxys into its Attack Forme. Unova Nacrene Museum A singular meteorite on display in the upper-left corner of the ground floor. It allows a singular Deoxys to cycle between all of its forms. Kalos Fossil Lab Five meteorites on a shelf at the back of the lab. All five meteorites can allow a singular Deoxys to cycle between any of its forms. Hoenn OR AS Fallarbor Town A singular meteorite on display at the back of Professor Cozmo 's house . It allows a singular Deoxys to cycle between all of its forms. Alola Hokulani Observatory A singular meteorite displayed besides Sophocles in the back room of the observatory. It allows a singular Deoxys to cycle between all of its forms. Appearance [ edit | edit source ] Veilstone City Pokémon Diamond, Pearl , and Platinum Route 3 Pokémon HeartGold and SoulSilver Nacrene Museum Pokémon Black, White , Black 2, and White 2 Fossil Lab Pokémon X and Y Fallarbor Town Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire Hokulani Observatory Pokémon Sun, Moon , Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon Veilstone City Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl
